## The Code: FFN in Python

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="activations"}
```

GELU is the activation function used in GPT-2.
For large positive $x$, GELU approaches $x$; for large negative $x$, it approaches 0.
`gelu_matrix` applies the scalar GELU to every element of a matrix.

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="add_bias"}
```

`add_bias` broadcasts a bias vector $b$ across all rows of a matrix $M$.

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="make_feed_forward"}
```

`FeedForward` holds the four parameters.
`make_feed_forward` allocates them: $W_1$ expands the model dimension $d$ to $4d$, and $W_2$ contracts it back.

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="feed_forward"}
```

`feed_forward` composes the two linear layers with GELU: expand, activate, contract.

```{.python include="src/python/chapter_demos.py" snippet="chapter_09"}
```

The demo applies one FFN to a three-token sequence and returns the unchanged outer shape.
Run it with `python3 src/python/chapter_demos.py`.
